search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng38 non-complex fractures assessment documentation information
site:who.int basic emergency care extremity trauma perfusion
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic fracture diagnosis or imaging selection
analgesia, immobilisation, reduction and referral instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A suspected fracture requires clinician assessment and often imaging.
No treatment, procedure, medicine, dose or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with suspected non-complex fracture; open, pelvic, spinal, skull, pathological and major-trauma injuries need separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Emergency assessment of suspected non-complex fracture.
UAE_applicability: Assessment principles transfer, but UAE trauma, imaging, orthopaedic, safeguarding and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG38 (reviewed 2025) and WHO/ICRC BEC exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NICE NG38 remains current; WHO/ICRC BEC remains the registered source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE non-complex-fracture assessment section supports mechanism, onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and manual focused assessment without confirming a fracture.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO/ICRC extremity-trauma section supports recording vital data, appearance and time-critical abnormalities only when assessed; it does not auto-state neurovascular status.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact documentation and information section supports recording existing imaging, clinician plan, completed discussion and arranged handover only as clinician-confirmed facts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Management recommendations were reviewed only to delimit clinician-documented actions; no immobilisation, analgesia, manipulation or referral is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.

unresolved_source_gaps: Anatomical site, deformity, skin, neurovascular detail, open injury, imaging choice and result, diagnosis, treatment, procedure, medicine, dose and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
